key_insight: The paper combines semi-smooth Newton reduced-order modeling with neural-network corrections and hyper-reduction to efficiently solve nonlinear parametric variational inequalities.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster surrogate simulation of contact and obstacle mechanics problems while retaining accuracy for deployment in computational mechanics workflows.
method: Build POD reduced bases for both primal and dual variables from snapshot data.
Augment the reduced coordinates with two feedforward networks to learn nonlinear manifold corrections.
Apply hyper-reduction via sparse cubature and greedy nonnegative least squares to reduce online residual cost.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports evaluation on a 2D obstacle problem and a 3D frictional contact problem, comparing full-order, linear reduced, neural-augmented, and hyper-reduced variants with substantial online cost reduction.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of snapshot coverage and on how well the hyper-reduction preserves the learned nonlinear manifold during Newton iterations.
paper_title: A Hyper-Reduced Neural Network-Augmented Semi-smooth Newton Method for Nonlinear Parametric Variational Inequalities
